message:
feat: add SyntaxHighlighter API and expand rendering infrastructure

Major additions to the ftui-extras and ftui-render crates to support
syntax highlighting and improved text rendering.

Syntax Highlighting (ftui-extras/src/syntax.rs +744 lines):
- Add SyntaxHighlighter struct combining TokenizerRegistry + HighlightTheme
- Convert code to styled ftui_text::Text with colored spans
- Add names() method to TokenizerRegistry for introspection
- Support for language detection by file extension
- Configurable themes with semantic token styling

Rendering Infrastructure (ftui-render):
- Add grapheme_pool.rs (+153 lines) for efficient grapheme cluster handling
- Reduce memory allocations through grapheme pooling/interning
- Add presenter.rs (+121 lines) for structured presentation layer
- Update frame.rs with improved region handling

Harness Updates (ftui-harness):
- Expand main.rs (+340 lines) with new demonstration widgets
- Add syntax highlighting demos to harness
- Integrate new rendering capabilities

E2E Test Improvements:
- Enhance run_all.sh with better test orchestration
- Expand test_altscreen.sh, test_cleanup.sh, test_inline.sh, test_input.sh
- Add more comprehensive terminal mode testing scenarios

Widget Updates:
- Update virtualized.rs scroll behavior (+50 lines)
- Adjust log_ring.rs capacity handling
- Expand widget benchmarks for performance validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-3bp.13","title":"Rich Renderables Test Suite","description":"Comprehensive test suite for all rich_rust renderables. Ensures correct rendering, measurement, and styling across all components.

## Testing Philosophy
Renderables require three types of verification:
1. **Measurement correctness** - min/max width calculations
2. **Rendering correctness** - Output matches expected segments
3. **Style propagation** - Styles cascade properly through nesting

## Test Requirements by Renderable

### Align (bd-3bp.1)
Unit tests:
- `test_align_left_no_change` - Left alignment is identity
- `test_align_center_even_width` - Symmetric padding for even widths
- `test_align_center_odd_width` - Extra space goes right
- `test_align_right_full_pad` - All padding on left
- `test_align_justify_word_spacing` - Even word spacing
- `test_align_vertical_middle` - Correct top/bottom padding
- `test_align_custom_fill_char` - Non-space fill characters

Snapshot tests:
- `snapshot_align_all_modes` - Visual grid of all alignment modes

### Columns (bd-3bp.2)
Unit tests:
- `test_columns_equal_width` - Each column gets 1/N of space
- `test_columns_ratio_distribution` - Proportional width allocation
- `test_columns_fixed_width` - Fixed columns don't flex
- `test_columns_auto_width` - Auto columns shrink to content
- `test_columns_gutter_applied` - Correct spacing between columns
- `test_columns_overflow_truncate` - Content truncated when too wide
- `test_columns_height_alignment` - Shorter columns padded to match tallest

Property tests:
- `prop_total_width_matches` - Sum of column widths + gutters = total
- `prop_no_overlap` - Column content areas don't overlap

### Emoji (bd-3bp.3)
Unit tests:
- `test_emoji_lookup_valid` - Known emoji names resolve
- `test_emoji_lookup_invalid` - Unknown names return None
- `test_emoji_width_standard` - Most emoji are 2 cells
- `test_emoji_width_variation` - Variation selectors handled
- `test_emoji_zwj_family` - Family emoji width correct
- `test_emoji_skin_tone` - Skin tone modifiers work
- `test_emoji_shortcode_replace` - :thumbsup: -> 👍

Corpus tests:
- `corpus_all_emoji` - Test all emoji in database

### Group (bd-3bp.4)
Unit tests:
- `test_group_single_item` - Single item renders unchanged
- `test_group_multiple_items` - Items stacked vertically
- `test_group_with_separator` - Separator between each pair
- `test_group_measurement_max` - Width is max of all children
- `test_group_measurement_sum` - Height is sum of all children
- `test_group_nested` - Groups can contain groups

### JSON (bd-3bp.5)
Unit tests:
- `test_json_primitive_string` - String with quotes and escapes
- `test_json_primitive_number` - Integer and float formatting
- `test_json_primitive_bool` - true/false styling
- `test_json_primitive_null` - null styling
- `test_json_object_empty` - {} formatting
- `test_json_object_single_key` - No trailing comma
- `test_json_object_multiple_keys` - Commas between entries
- `test_json_array_inline` - Small arrays on one line
- `test_json_array_multiline` - Large arrays split across lines
- `test_json_nested_deep` - Correct indentation at depth
- `test_json_invalid_fallback` - Invalid JSON shows error

Snapshot tests:
- `snapshot_json_complex_document` - Full document rendering

### Layout (bd-3bp.6)
Unit tests:
- `test_layout_single_cell` - One item fills grid
- `test_layout_multiple_rows` - Items wrap to next row
- `test_layout_column_span` - Item spans multiple columns
- `test_layout_row_span` - Item spans multiple rows
- `test_layout_gap_applied` - Row and column gaps
- `test_layout_auto_sizing` - Auto columns fit content
- `test_layout_fr_distribution` - Fr units divide remaining space

Property tests:
- `prop_no_cell_overlap` - Grid cells don't overlap
- `prop_bounds_respected` - All content within grid bounds

### Padding (bd-3bp.7)
Unit tests:
- `test_padding_all` - Equal padding on all sides
- `test_padding_horizontal` - Left/right only
- `test_padding_vertical` - Top/bottom only
- `test_padding_individual` - Each side different
- `test_padding_measurement` - Min/max adjusted by padding
- `test_padding_style_applied` - Padding area styled

### Panel (bd-3bp.8)
Unit tests:
- `test_panel_rounded_border` - Rounded corners render
- `test_panel_square_border` - Square corners render
- `test_panel_double_border` - Double-line border
- `test_panel_ascii_fallback` - ASCII-only border chars
- `test_panel_title_left` - Title at top-left
- `test_panel_title_center` - Title centered in top border
- `test_panel_title_truncation` - Long title truncated with ellipsis
- `test_panel_subtitle` - Subtitle on bottom border
- `test_panel_content_padding` - Content inset from border

Snapshot tests:
- `snapshot_panel_all_styles` - Grid of all border styles

### Pretty (bd-3bp.9)
Unit tests:
- `test_pretty_primitive_types` - i32, f64, bool, char
- `test_pretty_string_quoted` - Strings show quotes
- `test_pretty_vec_inline` - Small vecs on one line
- `test_pretty_vec_multiline` - Large vecs split
- `test_pretty_struct` - Named fields with values
- `test_pretty_enum_unit` - Variant name only
- `test_pretty_enum_tuple` - Variant with tuple args
- `test_pretty_depth_limit` - Stops at max depth with ...
- `test_pretty_string_limit` - Long strings truncated

### Rule (bd-3bp.10)
Unit tests:
- `test_rule_full_width` - Fills available width
- `test_rule_with_title` - Title centered in line
- `test_rule_title_left` - Title at left edge
- `test_rule_custom_char` - Non-default line character
- `test_rule_ascii_fallback` - Uses - instead of ─

### Traceback (bd-3bp.11)
Unit tests:
- `test_traceback_single_frame` - One stack frame
- `test_traceback_multiple_frames` - Stack of frames
- `test_traceback_with_source` - Source code context
- `test_traceback_without_source` - Graceful degradation
- `test_traceback_chained_cause` - Caused by: chain
- `test_traceback_frame_filter` - Hide stdlib frames
- `test_traceback_hyperlinks` - File paths are clickable

### Tree (bd-3bp.12)
Unit tests:
- `test_tree_single_node` - Root only
- `test_tree_single_child` - Root with one child
- `test_tree_multiple_children` - Root with siblings
- `test_tree_deep_nesting` - Multi-level hierarchy
- `test_tree_guide_last_child` - └── for last child
- `test_tree_guide_middle_child` - ├── for middle children
- `test_tree_collapsed_node` - Collapsed indicator
- `test_tree_ascii_fallback` - Uses +-- instead of ├──

Snapshot tests:
- `snapshot_tree_filesystem` - Typical directory structure

## Measurement Protocol Tests
For all renderables:
- `test_measure_returns_valid` - min <= max
- `test_measure_respects_constraint` - max <= max_width param
- `test_render_fits_measurement` - Rendered output <= measured width

## Logging Requirements
- `tracing::debug!` for measurement decisions
- `tracing::debug!` for style resolution
- `tracing::trace!` for segment construction
- Measurement cache hit/miss logged at trace level

Dependencies: All renderables, Testing infrastructure (bd-10i.11)


## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Snapshot tests cover each renderable’s core behaviors and edge cases.
- [ ] Tests are deterministic and provide readable diffs on failure.
- [ ] The suite runs in CI and blocks regressions for Phase 5  {"id":"bd-3bp.2","title":"Implement Columns renderable (column layout)","description":"# Implement Columns renderable (column layout)

## Objective
Implement a column layout container that places children side-by-side with configurable spacing and width allocation.

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/renderables/columns.rs`

## Scope
- Place N children in horizontal columns.
- Support:
  - fixed spacing between columns
  - proportional or constraint-based width allocation (delegate to ftui Flex where possible)
- Deterministic rendering order.

## Integration Notes
- Prefer to use `bd-yzu` (Flex) for width allocation rather than implementing a second constraint solver.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for width allocation and rect computation.
- Snapshot tests for 2/3/4-column layouts under narrow and wide terminals.

- [ ] Width allocation is deterministic and uses ftui layout primitives.
- [ ] Unit + snapshot tests cover representative cases.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Gutter spacing between columns
- [ ] Per-column padding
- [ ] Expand mode fills available width

- [ ] Content truncated when column too narrow
- [ ] Vertical alignment within row
- [ ] Shorter columns padded to match tallest
- [ ] No content overlap between columns

### Measurement Requirements
- [ ] Minimum = sum of column minimums + gutters
- [ ] Maximum = sum of column maximums + gutters

### Logging Requirements

- [ ] tracing::trace! for column measurement
